authority.optimisticConcurrency: expected row_version compared in a transaction
authority.idempotency: durable learner_draft_mutation receipt per UUID request
authority.identityBoundary: session-derived user plus HMAC-derived user-and-session cache namespace
authority.curriculumScope: active/completed enrollment in a beta/verified course and lesson skill
authority.languageIdentity: code draft key includes implementation language; lesson notes use null
authority.aggregateQuota: 512 records and 33554432 UTF-8 bytes per learner, serialized in the repository and mirrored by a PostgreSQL trigger

provedBehaviors: Cache eviction or a new device namespace restores server-synced work from PostgreSQL.
Another learner or durable session namespace cannot address the prior browser cache.
Offline edits are visibly local-only and retain one request UUID until an unambiguous acknowledgement.
An old accepted request replayed after newer work does not duplicate or overwrite the newer row.
Two concurrent writes at one expected version produce exactly one winner and one explicit conflict.
A revoked/expired session or closed-book gate purges every cache entry in the denied session namespace and blocks draft synchronization without touching another namespace.
Failed sign-out preserves dirty offline-only text; confirmed sign-out synchronously clears all LearnCoding draft entries before navigation.
C++, Java, Python, and other code facets cannot overwrite one another for the same DSA skill.
Arbitrary/unpublished/unenrolled scopes fail closed before draft reads or writes.
Concurrent writes cannot exceed the per-user record quota; the byte and record checks exist at both repository and database-trigger boundaries.
Export schema 4 includes drafts and content-free mutation history; deletion removes receipts before drafts; retention v3 keeps both until account deletion.
No service worker, web-app manifest, Workbox dependency, background sync, or install surface exists.

verification.notes: Disposable PostgreSQL 17; all migrations through 0024 applied twice; includes ownership, scope, DSA language isolation, idempotency, optimistic concurrency, database-trigger quota concurrency, cascade, and constraint cases.

verification.command: npm run lint && npm run typecheck
npm run test:auth-boundary && npm run security:api-surface

verification.notes: The recorded use-synced-draft hash includes the final dependency-array warning fix.
Revalidated after the Patch mentor cockpit and responsive lesson-workspace interaction update; the dedicated lesson-workspace interaction suite passed 9 of 9 tests.

remainingLimitations: Offline support is warm-page/browser-session only; a cold offline launch is impossible by design because the product is not an installable PWA.
Unsynced local-only text can be lost if sessionStorage is evicted before synchronization; the UI states this explicitly.

A disconnected server cannot remotely execute JavaScript to erase old sessionStorage after account deletion; server access is revoked immediately and normal logout/next denied request clears the current namespace.
No deployed Cloudflare/real-cookie multi-device browser penetration run has been retained; evidence here is local unit, browser-contract, route, lifecycle, and real-PostgreSQL integration evidence.
